title: brassica spinescens var. scopulorum
intro: Brassica spinescens var. scopulorum, also known as Rocky Mountain mustard, is a biennial plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Brassica spinescens var. scopulorum is often prepared as a tea or infused oil for its herbal uses.
